title: The Invisible Root Where Drainage Becomes Landscape

features.name: the root mouth
features.desc-on-touch: Where the outfall becomes soil, the earth opens to receive what the town refuses to acknowledge. The drainage learns to grow.

features.name: the buried channel
features.desc-on-touch: A groove in the earth where water once ran visible. Now it runs beneath everything, shaping the land from underneath. The scrub grows only where it flows.

features.name: scrub that listens
features.desc-on-touch: The bushes here grow thicker, listening to the invisible water beneath. They are the archive's secondary recording—the landscape becoming memory.

inscription: what the town flushes becomes the hidden root; the landscape grows on what it refuses to see; the drainage has learned to speak only to the scrub

file.name: Runoff_Trace_Soil_Composition_Root_Depth_Study.txt
file.note: A hydrological study of where the town's water goes. The data is incomplete—measurements stop where the channel goes invisible. The scrub was not measured.

reverie.title: REVERIE — the outfall root at midday, when invisible things
narr: you kneel and dig with leif, the earth soft where
the drainage flows beneath. 'the town doesn't know
it's a root now', leif says. the scrub hums with
it—insects, plants, all the things that live on what
the city forgets. you touch the wet soil. the outfall

exits.label: back to the outfall where voices fade into plumbing
deeper into the scrub where the root widens
following the invisible channel upstream toward the town
